- [x] Verify that the copilot-instructions.md file in the .github directory is created.

- [x] Clarify Project Requirements – Ask for stack details unless the user already supplied them.

- [x] Scaffold the Project – Use the project setup tooling and work in the root directory.

- [x] Customize the Project – Plan updates, modify code per requirements, and skip only for "Hello World" scope.

- [x] Install Required Extensions – Only install extensions explicitly listed by setup tooling; otherwise mark as complete.

- [x] Compile the Project – Install dependencies, run diagnostics, and resolve build issues before moving on.

- [x] Create and Run Task – Add a VS Code task if helpful for this workspace; document it when created.

- [ ] Launch the Project – Only launch in debug mode when the user confirms they are ready.

- [x] Ensure Documentation is Complete – Keep README and this checklist up to date; remove stale guidance.

Execution guidelines:
- Track progress with the todo list and update it before each new step.
- Keep communication concise, avoid dumping large command outputs, and skip structure explanations unless asked.
- Run commands from the project root, avoid unnecessary media, and point out placeholders when used.
- Do not install unrequested extensions and prefer clarified requirements over assumptions.
- Consider the work done when the project builds cleanly, documentation is current, and launch/debug instructions are provided.
- Work through each checklist item systematically, keep communication concise, and follow development best practices.
